title: Postman Collection (minimal coherence schema)
description: api-tests/collections/[story-id].postman_collection.json. This is NOT the full Postman Collection v2.1 schema (that is huge). It is a minimal schema that guarantees what the API Agent generates is structurally coherent: a named collection in the v2.1 format, a non-empty item array, every request-item carrying a request, and any test scripts being well-formed. Traceability IDs (COL-XXX in info.name, REQ-XXX + the originating TC in each item) are enforced by agents/api-agent.md and the human at Gate 3/4, not by this schema.

name.description: Human-readable collection name. By convention carries the COL-XXX id and the story id, e.g. 'COL-001 api-create-user'.

schema.description: The Postman collection format URL. Must be the v2.1.0 schema.

item.description: Non-empty. Each entry is either a request-item (has a request) or a folder-item (has a nested item array).

event.description: Optional. Pre-request and test scripts. Items with assertions carry a 'test' event whose script.exec is the assertion code.

oneOf.description: Folder-item: a group containing nested items.

request.description: A Postman request. May be the shorthand string form or the object form; the object form should carry at least a method and url.

url.description: Postman accepts a url as a string or a structured object. Credentials and the base URL come from the environment via {{variables}}, never hardcoded.

exec.description: The script body. Postman stores it as an array of lines or a single string.
